title: hemerocallis fulva var. angustifolia
intro: Hemerocallis fulva var. angustifolia, also known as the Orange Daylily, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the lily family. The roots and underground parts of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic effects. The health benefits of Hemerocallis fulva var. angustifolia include treating various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin issues, as well as supporting kidney health and lowering blood pressure. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used topically to apply its antiseptic properties directly to the skin.
